title: Hierarchical stint-hazard priors instead of fixed compound life tables
summary: The current best features hard-code generic compound life windows.
rationale: The current best features hard-code generic compound life windows. Discrete-time survival work supports estimating per-interval hazard, and F1 pit-stop papers frame the decision as lap-level event timing rather than a single static threshold.
implementation_hint: Build out-of-fold empirical/Bayesian hazard tables for pit-on-next-lap using keys like `Compound x Stint x TyreLife`, then shrink toward `Race x Compound`, `Year x Compound`, and global priors. Feed prior hazard, cumulative survival-to-finish, and prior-vs-observed residual features into the main classifier.
expected_effect: Should outperform hand-set life constants by learning track- and regime-specific stopping curves while remaining tabular and interpretable.
risk: Easy to leak if priors are fit on the full dataset or include later laps from the same driver-race in validation folds.
